title: Multi-Fidelity Computational Screening of High-Entropy MBenes for CO$_2$ Electroreduction
authors: Sree Harsha Bharadwaj H, Raghavan Ranganathan
abstract: High-entropy MBenes (HE-MBenes) represent a promising, unexplored class of 2D materials for electrocatalysis. In this work, we present a systematic computational screening of 56 equiatomic quinary HE-MBene compositions from the {Ti, V, Cr, Mo, Nb, Ta, Zr, Hf} pool for CO$_2$ adsorption and electroreduction. Using the Monte Carlo Special Quasirandom Structure (MCSQS) algorithm, we generated disordered M$_1B_1$-type supercells and assessed structural stability via DFT (PBE+D3) in VASP. Of the 56 candidates, 55 passed relaxation, with 45 exhibiting negative formation energies, confirming thermodynamic stability. To efficiently screen CO$_2$ adsorption across disordered surfaces, we developed a machine-learning interatomic potential (MLIP) using the MACE architecture. Fine-tuned on our DFT dataset, the model achieved energy RMSEs of 3.49 and 3.0 meV/atom for adsorbed and pristine sets, respectively. Active sites were identified via PDOS analysis, matching metal d-orbital signatures with CO$_2$ molecular orbitals. The rate-determining step of the CO$_2$-to-CO pathway was evaluated using the computational hydrogen electrode (CHE) model. Short-time structural integrity was assessed via AIMD at 500 K over 2.5 ps; phonon-based stability remains a priority for future work. Our results establish an integrated DFT-MLIP-AIMD framework for the rational design of high-entropy 2D materials tailored for CO$_2$ conversion.
summary: This paper presents a computational framework to discover new 2D high-entropy materials for CO2 conversion. By integrating machine learning potentials with DFT, the authors can rapidly screen complex, disordered surfaces for catalytic efficiency. The study successfully identifies a large set of stable candidates for electrocatalysis.
main_problem: The study aims to efficiently screen a large chemical space of high-entropy MBenes for their potential as electrocatalysts for CO2 reduction.
main_result: The researchers identified 45 thermodynamically stable high-entropy MBenes and developed a highly accurate MACE-based machine-learning interatomic potential for rapid adsorption screening.
method: The work uses a multi-fidelity approach combining Monte Carlo Special Quasirandom Structure (MCSQS) for disorder, DFT (PBE+D3) for stability, MACE machine-learning interatomic potentials (MLIP), and AIMD.
model_or_system: The system consists of 56 equiatomic quinary high-entropy MBenes (HE-MBenes) composed of elements from the {Ti, V, Cr, Mo, Nb, Ta, Zr, Hf} pool, modeled as disordered M1B1-type 2D materials.
key_observables: Formation energy, CO2 adsorption energy, energy RMSE of the MLIP, and PDOS-based active site identification.
important_parameters: Temperature of 500 K for AIMD; 2.5 ps simulation timescale; equiatomic composition.
main_assumptions: The stability of the structures is assumed based on formation energy and short-time AIMD, though phonon-based stability is noted as a required future step.
paper_structure: The paper follows a workflow of: generating disordered structures via MCSQS, assessing thermodynamic stability via DFT, developing and validating a MACE machine-learning potential, analyzing catalytic activity via PDOS and CHE models, and verifying short-term stability via AIMD.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
